subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference
- **Standard game** — 3 to 20 players.
- **Recommended** — 6 players.

#### Core Components
- **Photo cards** — 75 photo/meme image cards showing popular meme templates and funny images.
- **Caption cards** — 435 caption cards with humorous text to pair with photos.
- **Easel** — 1 easel/stand to display the current photo card.
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.

#### Card Details
- **Photo cards** — Large format cards featuring meme-worthy images.
- **Caption cards** — Standard size cards with funny captions, phrases, or sentences.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Shuffle photo cards** — Shuffle the photo card deck and place it face-down in the center.
2. **Shuffle caption cards** — Shuffle the caption card deck.
3. **Deal caption cards** — Deal 7 caption cards to each player.
4. **Set up easel** — Place the easel where all players can see it.
5. **Select first judge** — Choose the first judge by any method.

#### Table Layout
- **Photo deck** — Face-down near the easel.
- **Caption deck** — Central draw pile.
- **Easel** — Displays the current round's photo card.
- **Submission area** — Space for players to place caption cards face-down.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Roles
- **Judge** — 1 player per round; rotates clockwise each round.
- **Players** — All other players submit caption cards.

#### Hand Management
- **Starting hand** — 7 caption cards.
- **Replenishment** — Draw back to 7 after each round.
- **Judge's hand** — Judge keeps their hand but does not submit during their judging turn.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Pre-First-Turn Checks
- **All players have 7 caption cards** — Verify hand size.
- **Judge selected** — First judge is identified.
- **Easel ready** — Easel is set up and visible.

#### First Action
1. **Reveal photo card** — Judge draws the top photo card and places it on the easel.
2. **Players submit** — All other players select a caption card from their hand to create the funniest meme.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Flow
1. **Reveal photo** — Judge draws a photo card and places it on the easel for all to see.
2. **Submit captions** — All other players choose one caption card from their hand and place it face-down.
3. **Shuffle submissions** — Judge collects and shuffles all submitted caption cards.
4. **Read aloud** — Judge reads each caption card aloud while displaying it next to the photo.
5. **Judge picks winner** — Judge selects the funniest combination.
6. **Award point** — The winning player takes the photo card as a point.
7. **Draw up** — All players who submitted draw one caption card to return to 7.
8. **Rotate judge** — Next player clockwise becomes the judge.

#### Round Pacing
- **No time limit** — There is no timer; players submit when ready.
- **Speed variant** — Set a 60-second timer for submissions to keep the game moving.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Player Actions
- **Select caption** — Choose the caption card from your hand that pairs best with the photo.
- **Submit face-down** — Place your chosen card face-down to keep it anonymous until the judge reads them.
- **Draw replacement** — After the round, draw from the caption deck to return to 7 cards.

#### Judge Actions
- **Reveal photo** — Draw and display the photo card.
- **Read submissions** — Read all caption cards aloud, pairing each with the photo.
- **Select winner** — Pick the funniest or best combination based on personal preference.
- **Judge is final** — No appeals; the judge's choice stands.

#### Card Management
- **Discard** — Non-winning caption cards go to a discard pile.
- **Reshuffle** — If the caption deck runs out, reshuffle the discard pile.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Judging Style
- **Subjective** — There is no "correct" answer. The judge picks based entirely on personal humor.
- **Reading delivery** — How the judge reads the captions can affect which gets the biggest laugh.

#### House Rules & Variants
- **Drafting variant** — Instead of random dealing, lay out caption cards and let players draft.
- **Themed rounds** — Restrict submissions to a theme (e.g., "only wholesome captions").
- **Audience voting** — Instead of a single judge, all players vote (judge breaks ties).
- **Marathon mode** — Play until the photo deck runs out instead of to a point target.

#### Adult Content
- **Mature humor** — The game contains adult content and is intended for players 17+.
- **Content filter** — Groups may pre-screen and remove cards they find too offensive.

#### Expansion Compatibility
- **Expansion packs** — Many themed expansion packs add additional photo and caption cards.
- **Mix and match** — Expansion cards shuffle directly into the base game decks.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- **Point target** — Play until a player reaches an agreed number of photo cards (commonly 5-7).
- **Alternatively** — Play for a set number of rounds or until the photo deck runs out.

#### Scoring Model
- **Photo cards = points** — Each photo card won equals 1 point.
- **Most points wins** — Player with the most photo cards at game end wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **No official tiebreaker** — In case of a tie, play a sudden-death round with all tied players submitting to a neutral judge.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Know Your Judge
- **Read the room** — Pay attention to what makes the current judge laugh hardest.
- **Humor style** — Some judges prefer absurd non-sequiturs; others prefer clever or dark humor.
- **Consistency** — Track judge preferences across rounds to predict future picks.

#### Hand Management
- **Save versatile captions** — Keep caption cards that work with many different photo types.
- **Burn weak cards** — Play your weakest caption when the judge's taste doesn't match your hand.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Playing your own favorite** — Submitting what YOU think is funniest instead of what the JUDGE will pick.
- **Overthinking** — The funniest caption is often the first one that makes you laugh, not the cleverest.
- **Ignoring the photo** — Submitting a generically funny caption that does not connect specifically to the image.
- **Hoarding good captions** — Waiting for the "perfect" photo match that may never come.
- **Monotone reading (as judge)** — A flat delivery kills the comedy. Good judges sell the captions with dramatic reads.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Specific vs. Generic Caption
- **Specific** — A caption that references something particular about the photo image. Higher impact when it lands.
- **Generic** — A broadly funny caption. Safer but less memorable.

#### Decision 2: Safe vs. Edgy
- **Safe humor** — Universally inoffensive; works with most judges.
- **Edgy humor** — Higher risk but can dominate with the right judge.

#### Decision 3: As Judge
- **First laugh wins** — Pick the caption that got the biggest immediate reaction.
- **Slow burn** — Sometimes a subtler caption is funnier the more you think about it.
